label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's break down this question about causation and intentionality. The scenario involves a hunter who is in a competition and sees a large deer. If he can kill this deer, he will win. He aims at the deer, but at the last moment, he notices there are bird-watchers on the other side of the deer. Despite this, he chooses to shoot the deer, not caring about the bird-watchers. The result is that the deer is killed, and one of the bird-watchers is also hit. The question is whether the man intentionally shot
